native_api_think_false.note: control: /api/chat with think:false — thinking GENUINELY off (content='391', thinking_len=0, done_reason=stop, eval_count=4). The param and mechanism are right; the /v1 OpenAI-compat path drops the field.

adjudication: All three /v1 arms are behaviorally IDENTICAL under the tiny-cap discriminator (max_tokens=60): reasoning generated (205-256 chars), empty content, finish_reason=length, all 60 completion_tokens consumed reasoning-first. think:false did not suppress generation-level thinking, nor even the reasoning field. chat_template_kwargs{enable_thinking:false} equally ineffective (supplementary). The native /api/chat control PROVES the mechanism works one path over — the /v1 compat layer silently ignores the top-level think field. Typed outcome: no-op. Per the spec's terminal-close invariant this is NO_OP_BLOCKED: the thinking A/B cannot construct a thinking-off arm through the gateway as wired; reconciliation (e.g. routing think via the native API path) is a follow-up revision, never a silent re-point.
